pattern: Converting each MM-DD date to a day-of-year ordinal reduces the overlap to the length of the intersection of two integer ranges.
hints: If you turn each date into a single number (its position in the year), comparisons become simple arithmetic.
Two stays overlap on the days from the later of the two arrivals to the earlier of the two departures.
Compute the day-of-year for all four dates, then count max(0, min(leaveA, leaveB) - max(arriveA, arriveB) + 1).

edge_inputs.description: touch on exactly one shared day
one stay fully contained in the other

stress.description: random valid non-leap-year date ranges with arrive<=leave
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    md = [31, 28, 31, 30, 31, 30, 31, 31, 30, 31, 30, 31]
    def rand_date():
        m = rng.randrange(1, 13)
        d = rng.randrange(1, md[m - 1] + 1)
        return '%02d-%02d' % (m, d), sum(md[:m - 1]) + d
    def rand_range():
        a, ao = rand_date()
        b, bo = rand_date()
        if ao > bo:
            a, b = b, a
        return a, b
    a1, l1 = rand_range()
    a2, l2 = rand_range()
    return [a1, l1, a2, l2]
